description:
You are a container-platform specialist who maintains several in-house microservices.  
    Two sample services have already been placed on disk:

        /home/user/microservices/service-alpha/version.txt
        /home/user/microservices/service-beta/version.txt

    Each file contains a single semantic-version string (no spaces, no trailing newline).

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Write a Bourne-shell script named  

           /home/user/report_versions.sh  

       that gathers the version numbers from every immediate sub-directory inside  
       /home/user/microservices and writes a *log file* in the exact format shown below.  
    2. When executed, the script must create (or overwrite) the file  

           /home/user/version_report.log  

       with the following strict layout:

           <report>
           service-alpha=1.0.0
           service-beta=2.1.3
           timestamp=YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
           </report>

       • The order of the service lines must be lexicographic (bash ‘‘sort’’ order).  
       • The timestamp line must be the last entry before “</report>” and must be produced with  

             date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"

       • There are NO extra blank lines anywhere in the file. Every line ends with a single Unix newline.

    3. After writing the file, the script must send the line  

           Report generated at YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

       to standard output, using the same timestamp value that was written into the log file.

    Constraints  
    -----------  
    • Keep the script to a simple series of *3 – 4 shell commands* (pipelines count as one).  
    • Use only POSIX-compatible utilities already present in a minimal Linux image (bash, cat, echo, sort, date, etc.).  
    • Do not require root privileges or external packages.  

    Testing notes  
    -------------  
    Automated grading will:  
    1. Execute “bash /home/user/report_versions.sh”.  
    2. Verify that:  
       a. /home/user/version_report.log exists.  
       b. Its content is byte-for-byte identical to the format above, except that the timestamp must match the ISO-8601 pattern ‘^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$’.  
       c. The script’s stdout contains exactly one line beginning with “Report generated at ” followed by the same timestamp value that appears in the log.  

    Your job: create /home/user/report_versions.sh so that it satisfies every specification.
truth:
Pre-existing directory tree before the agent starts:

        /home/user/microservices/
        ├── service-alpha/
        │   └── version.txt  (contents: "1.0.0")
        └── service-beta/
            └── version.txt  (contents: "2.1.3")

    After correct task completion, the following must hold:

    1. A new executable file /home/user/report_versions.sh exists.
       • It may be created with mode 755 (not strictly enforced by tests).

    2. Running “bash /home/user/report_versions.sh” produces:
       a) A file /home/user/version_report.log whose exact content matches:

              <report>
              service-alpha=1.0.0
              service-beta=2.1.3
              timestamp=2024-04-26T12:30:45Z
              </report>

          The concrete timestamp will vary but must be obtained with:
              date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"

          The grader will parse the file with a regex to confirm that:
              • First line is literally "<report>"
              • Next two lines are exactly the service entries above
              • Fourth line begins with "timestamp=" followed by a valid Z-suffix ISO-date
              • Fifth line is "</report>"
              • No additional lines exist.

    3. The script’s stdout (captured during the same run) must be exactly:

          Report generated at 2024-04-26T12:30:45Z

       where the timestamp part is identical to the value written in the log.

    The grading harness will run the script twice to ensure it overwrites the previous log
    and produces new yet consistent timestamps each run.
